alt: Matt Headley overlooking the raised garden beds and gazebo at Longleaf Botanical Gardens
caption: The old pool area — I used to walk the trails here for sermon planning, reading, and prayer.

alt: Welcome sign and trail map at the entrance to Longleaf Botanical Gardens
caption: The welcome sign at the gardens entrance — most people still drive right past it.

alt: Museum staff holding a chinchilla for a group of children during a birthday party at the Anniston Museum of Natural History
caption: My kids had birthday parties here — museum staff introducing the group to Gus the chinchilla.

alt: Venus flytrap and pitcher plants in a raised bed at Longleaf Botanical Gardens
caption: Venus flytraps and pitcher plants in the carnivorous plant bed — a kid magnet.

alt: Sensory Garden sign surrounded by blooming celosia, created by the Rotary Club of Anniston
caption: The Sensory Garden — a Rotary Club of Anniston project, celosia blazing in the fall.

alt: Koi swimming in a sun-dappled pond at the Anniston Museum of Natural History gardens
caption: The koi pond outside the museum — one of those spots you stumble into and don't want to leave.

alt: Matt Headley in front of blooming dahlias at Longleaf Botanical Gardens
caption: The dahlia bed in late September — worth the visit on its own.

alt: A child exploring the lobby of the Anniston Museum of Natural History
caption: My youngest exploring the museum lobby — the kind of place that makes a kid stop and look up.
